F,crn PolrrrcAr- PnACTrcps Co\.rMrssroN
-llS J Street r Suite 610 o Srcranrr.nro. CA c)-5lJl-l-l-ll9
l9l6t lll--5660 o Fux t9l(rt .tll-0336
October 1,2009
David Nelson
P.O. Box 2020
Grass Valley, CA 95945
Re
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. A-09-208
' The Political Retirrm Act is contained in Governnrent Code Sections til(X)0 through 9l0l+. All
statuk)ry ret'erences are to the Government Code. unless otherwise indicated. The re-eulationi of the Fair
Politicul Practices Commission are contained in Sections ltll I0 through llt997 of Title 2 of the Califirrnia
Code of Regulations. -\ll regulatory ret'erences are to Title l. Dil ision 6 of the Culitor.nia Clrtle of
Regul at io ns. unless otherr.r'ise i rrd icated.
t We have cttnsolidated and rearranged your questions tor ease of legal unalysis.
Dear Mr. Nelson:
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the post-govemmental
employment restrictions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").' ti,i.; lelter shor_rld not
be construed as advice on any conduct that may have already taken place. This letter is
based on the facts presented. The Fair Political Practices Commissibn ("Commission,,)
does not act as a finder of fact when it renders assistance. (ln re Oglesbl, (1975) 1 FppC
Ops.71; Gov. Code, g 83114.)
QUESTTONS2
After one year has elapsed from the date you left your state position at the
Department of Hor:sing and community Development (HCD), (where you recused
yourself from participating in any proceeding involving Nevada county), may yoll:
l. Work for Nevada County to operate loan programs, implement grants, or
facilitate loans that originated as HCD grants and for wtriitr yolr wrore grant applications
or submitted Program Income Reuse Plans while you were not employ.a Uy tlie state,l
2. Work for Nevada County to facilitate loans that originated as HCD grants
while you were employed by HCD if you recused yourself from participating in any HCD
grant decisions involving Nevada County'?


File No. A-09-208
Page No. 2
3. Contract with the City of Dixon to amend a "Program Income Reuse plan"
if you participated in HCD's decisions to approve (i) the HCD grant under which the
plan was adopted, and (ii) the original plan?
4. Administer Program Income for the City of Dixon if someone else prepares
the amended Program lncome Reuse Plan under which the Program Income *ltl b"
administered?
5. May you administer and operate Dixon's Revolving Loan Fund that originated
from a Community Development Block Grant ("CDBG") you approved while employed
by HCD?
CONCLUSIONS
1. The permanent ban applies only to a proceeding in which the former employee
participated while in state service. Accordingly, you may assist Nevada County with
operating loan programs, in implementing grants, or facilitating loans that originated as
HCD grants for grants that were made while you were not employed by the state.
2. Based on the fact that you recused yourself from participating in any
proceeding involving Nevada County while employed by HCD, you may facilitate these
loans that originated as HCD grants.
3. The permanent ban applies to a proceeding in which a former employee
participated while in state service. An amendment to a plan that contains nlw obligations
that did not exist under the original plan would not be the same proceeding. Because the
required provisions of an amended plan are substantially similar to those in an original
plan when viewed as a whole, they are considered to be the same proceeding and,
therefore, you may not work on the amended plan.
4. You may administer the Program Income if someone else handles the
amendment of the Program [ncome Reuse Plan. Administration of Program lncome
pursuant to a plan is considered a "new proceeding" separate from the drafting and
submission of the plan.
5. You may administer and operate the Revolving Loan Fund because this is a..
"new proceeding."
FACTSs
You retired from your state job at HCD on Septemb er 2. 2oog. At HCD you
participated in decisions to award -qrants to certain cities and counties through the CDBG
' Srtme facts recited in this letter are taken fiom your prior requests fbr advice in connectign with
our letters. I-08- 150 and A-09-0-5 l. the CDBG Grants Management Manual. which you have ret-erenced.
and our telephone conversation of September lj.2009.


File No. A-09-208
Page No. 3
program. However, while you were employed at HCD, you recused yourself from, and
had no involvement in, funding decisions involving Nevada County and jurisdictions
located within Nevada County.
Program Income Reuse Plans and Prograrn Income under CDBG
The CDBG program is a federal program established to make funds available to
eligible jurisdictions for development of viable communities by providing decent housing
and a suitable living environment and by expanding economic opportunities for persons
of low and moderate income. Under the program, HCD grants, (also referred to as
"CDBG grants"), are awarded to local jurisdictions, which then use the funds to make
loans to various individuals or entities. Proceeds from the repayment of these loans are
considered "Program [ncome." The local jurisdiction may then loan these funds again
pursuant to a Program Income Reuse Plan that must be approved by HCD. Once the plan
is approved, use of the funds is governed by the plan, rather than the original grant
agreement. Typically, the local jurisdiction places Program Income in a Revolving Loan
Fund from which future loans can be made to serve the purposes of the CDBG program.
Nevada County
You began your employment with HCD in 1990 and continued there until 2004,
at which time you went to work full time for Nevada County. Your work for Nevada
County involved writing grant applications for funds provided under the CDBG program
You returned to HCD in2006 and retired on September 1,200g.
Since retiring from state service, you have operated a consulting business, David
Nelson Consulting. Your business has entered into a contract to implement portions of
two existing CDBG grants awarded to Nevada County. Your services would consist of
coordinating and packaging individual loan applications, submitting them to HCD and
communicating with HCD staff regarding these submissions. You would do so by using
a CDBG checklist as a guide. You describe your work under this contract as ministerial.
You ask whether you may assist Nevada County in implementing existing grants under
this contract.
Since leaving state service, you have also provided grant application services for
Nevada County both as an unpaid volunteer and for compensation. Now that these grants
have been awarded, you ask whether you may contract with Nevada county as a
consultant to implement or facilitate these grants and the loans related to awards under
these grants.
Citlt of Di.ron
You would like to contract with the City of Dixon to amend a "Program Income
Reuse Plan" and to administer the "Program lncome" generated from the use of CDBG
firnds in accordance with the plan. The Program Income Reuse Plan must be approved
by HCD, both at the time of initial set-up and upon changing the use of the funds. you


File No. 4-09-208
Page No. "1
describe HCD approval to consist of using a checklist of "predetermined" items that are
set forth in the CDBG's Grants Management Manual (the "Manual"). You ask whether
you may assist in the amendment of a Program Income Reuse Plan, administer the related
Program lncome, and administer and operate the resulting Revolving Loan Fund if, as an
employee of HCD, yoLl approved the city's original plan, using the checklist. You also
ask whether yoll may administer the Program Income if someone else handles the
amendment of the plan. You have not indicated whether this other person would be
related to David Nelson Consulting.
ANALYSIS
As stated in our previous letter, officials who have left state service are subject to
two types of restrictions under the Act. Because it has been over a year since you left
state service, we limit our discussion to the permanent ban's prohibition on influencing
any judicial or other proceeding in which the official participated while in state service.a
The permanent ban prohibits a former state employee form "switching sides" and
participating, for compensation, in any specific proceeding involving the StaL of
California, or assisting others in such a proceeding, in which the former state employee
participated while employed by the stare. Sections 87401 andgT4oZ provide:
"No former state administrative official, after the termination of his or her
employment or term of office, shall for compensation act as agent or
attorney for, or otherwise represent, any other person (other than the state
of califomia) before any court or state administrative agency or any
officer or employee thereof by making any formal or informal appearance,
or by making any oral or written communication with the intent to
influence, in connection with any judicial, quasi-judicial or other
proceeding if both of the following apply:
"(a) The State of california is a party or has a direct and substantial
interest.
"(b) The proceeding is one in which the former state administrative
official participated." (Secrion 87 401.)
"No former state administrative official, after the termination of his or her
employment or term of office shall for compensation aid, advise, counsel,
consult or assist in representing any other person (except the State of
california) in any proceeding in which the official would be prohibited
from appearing under Section 87401." (Section S74OZ.)
'wediscussedtheprohibitionsoftheone-yearbaninourpriorletters(I-0g-l50and4-09-05 
l.t


File No. 4-09-208
Page No. 5
As stated in ottr previous letters, HCD is a state administrative agency.s As a
designated employee of HCD, yolr were considered a state administrativi ofiiciat.
The restriction of the permanent ban is limited to judicial, quasi-judicial, or other
proceedings in which you participated. Section 87400 provides:
"(c) 'Judicial, quasi-judicial or other proceeding' means any proceeding,
application, request for a ruling or other determination, contract, claim,
controversy, investigation, charge, accusation, arrest or other particular
matter involving a specific party or parties in any coLlrt or state
administrative agency, including but not limited to any proceeding
governed by chapter 5 (commencing with Secrion 11500) of Diviiion 3 of
Title2 of the Govemment Code.
"(d) 'Participated' means to have taken part personally and substantially
through decision, approval, disapproval, formal written recommendation,
rendering advice on a substantial basis, investigation or use of confidential
information as an officer or employee, but excluding approval,
disapproval or rendering of legal advisory opinions to dipurt-ental or
agency staff which do not involve a specific party or parties."
Under Section 87400(c), a "proceeding" includes any particular matter involving
a specific party or parties before a state administrative agency. The permanent ban does
not apply to a "new" proceeding even in cases where the new pro.."di.rg is related to or
grows out of a prior proceeding in which the official had partiiipated. A-..new',
proceeding not subject to the permanent ban typically involves different parties, subject
matter, or circumstances that differ from those involved in previous proceedings. (Ris/
Advice Letter, No. A-04-187; see arso Donovan Advice Leiter, No. i-o:-t 19.) New
contracts let by the employee's former agency, in which the former employeedid not
participate, are considered new proceedings. (Leslie Advice Letter, tto. l-dq-O+g.) A
"new" contract is one based on new consideration and new terms, even if it involves the
same parties. (Ferber Advice Letter, No. I-99-104; Andersoz Advice Letter, No. A-9g-
159.)
N
or
PI
employed by the state?
The permanent ban applies to a former govemmental official's appearance before
a colrt or state administrative agency if the official participated in a judicial, quasi-
judicial or other proceeding. (Section 87401). Under Section 87400id), ..partitipated"
' Section 87400(b) deflnes a "state administrative otllcial" as every member. otficer, emplgyee
or consultant of a state administrative a-cency who as part of his or her otficial responsibilities enga_ues in
any judicial. quasi-jtrdicial. or other proceeding in other than a purely clerical. secietarial. or ministerial
capacity.


File No. 4-09-208
Page No.6
means that the official took part "as an officer or employee" of the agency. Accordingly,
you are not barred from assisting Nevada County with implementing and facilitating
grants or operating loan programs resulting from the grants you wrote while employed by
Nevada County from 2004 to 2006 and after retiring from HCD in 2008 because you
were not a state employee at those times.
ln
Because you recLlsed yourself from all decisionmaking relating to Nevada County
applications while you worked for HCD, you did not "participate" in ony pro"..ding that
would bar you from facilitating loans administered by Nevada county.
In your capacity as an employee of HCD, you participated in decisions to approve
a CDBG grant and subsequently, a related Program Income Reuse Plan, for the City of
Dixon. The issue presented here is whether in preparing and submitting an amended
Program Income Reuse Plan, you would be working on that grant, or ii you would be
working on a "new proceeding." A related question is whether such work would be a
"new proceeding" separate from your approval of the original Program Income Reuse
Plan. [n order for you to represent the City of Dixon without violating Sections g74Ol
and87402, it would be necessary to find that the amended Program Income Reuse plan
constitutes a "new proceeding."
We first examine the relationship between the original Program Income Reuse
Plan and the amended plan. The Manual provides that new or revised program Income
Reuse Plans are to be drafted and submitted to the Department's CDBG Section for
review and approval to ensure that Program Income will be used for CDBG eligible
activities and will comply with CDBG National Objectives and federal overla/
requirements. The Manual states that applicants should refer to the checklist ,ifor a
guide" of the components of a Program Income Reuse Plan. It appears that both new and
revised plans are subject to the same requirements. In the past we have advised that if a
new contract sent out for re-bid is substantially the same as a current contract, that the
two contracts will be considered the same proceeding for purposes of the permanent ban.
(Anderson Advice Letter, supra-) If the two plans are substantially similai when viewed
as a whole, we would consider them to be the same proceeding. (Luccts Advice Letter,
No. I-05-160: Wartl Advice Letter 4-05-096.) The fact that the same criteria (ser forth in
the checklist) are used by CDBG in approving an original plan and an amended plan
suggests that the two plans are one proceeding. We conclude that you may not
participate in the preparation or submission of the amended plan bicause it is not a new
proceeding.
if


File No. A-09-208
Page No. 7
Because we have determined that you may not participate in the preparation or
submission of the amended plan, we need not address the question of whether the
amended plan is a new proceeding vis-ir-vis the original grant.
Ci
Plan
Program Income will be administered?
ln determining whether a proceeding is "new," we have found, generally, that
application, drafting and awarding of a contract, license or approval are different from
proceedings involving irnplementation of the same contract, license or approval. (Lucas
Advice Letter, supra: Anderson Advice Letter, slrpra; BlonienAdvice I-itter No. 89-463.)
We believe that administration of Program Income is comparable to implementation of a
contract, license or approval. Accordingly, if someone else prepares th; amended plan,
you may administer Program Income, whether pursllant to the original program Income
Reuse Plan or the amended plan, because.this is a new proceeding.6 -
The Revolving Loan Fund consists of Program Income that continues to be loaned
to various parties to serye the purposes of the CDBG program. As stated above, if
someone else prepares the amended Program Income Reuse Plan, you may administer
Program Income. The same rationale applies to your administering and operating the
Revolving Loan Fund. You may administer and operate the fund because this is a,.new
proceeding."
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
By: Valentina Joyce
Counsel, Legal Division
vJjgl

someone else fiom ytlur tirm were to prepare the amended plan. is your letter does not ,rgg.-rt that is the
case here.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: S2
After one year has elapsed from the date you left your state position at the
Department of Hor:sing and community Development (HCD), (where you recused
yourself from participating in any proceeding involving Nevada county), may yoll:
l. Work for Nevada County to operate loan programs, implement grants, or
facilitate loans that originated as HCD grants and for wtriitr yolr wrore grant applications
or submitted Program Income Reuse Plans while you were not employ.a Uy tlie state,l
2. Work for Nevada County to facilitate loans that originated as HCD grants
while you were employed by HCD if you recused yourself from participating in any HCD
grant decisions involving Nevada County'?
3. Contract with the City of Dixon to amend a "Program Income Reuse plan"
if you participated in HCD's decisions to approve (i) the HCD grant under which the
plan was adopted, and (ii) the original plan?
4. Administer Program Income for the City of Dixon if someone else prepares
the amended Program lncome Reuse Plan under which the Program Income *ltl b"
administered?
5. May you administer and operate Dixon's Revolving Loan Fund that originated
from a Community Development Block Grant ("CDBG") you approved while employed
by HCD?

CONCLUSION: 1. The permanent ban applies only to a proceeding in which the former employee
participated while in state service. Accordingly, you may assist Nevada County with
operating loan programs, in implementing grants, or facilitating loans that originated as
HCD grants for grants that were made while you were not employed by the state.
2. Based on the fact that you recused yourself from participating in any
proceeding involving Nevada County while employed by HCD, you may facilitate these
loans that originated as HCD grants.
3. The permanent ban applies to a proceeding in which a former employee
participated while in state service. An amendment to a plan that contains nlw obligations
that did not exist under the original plan would not be the same proceeding. Because the
required provisions of an amended plan are substantially similar to those in an original
plan when viewed as a whole, they are considered to be the same proceeding and,
therefore, you may not work on the amended plan.
4. You may administer the Program Income if someone else handles the
amendment of the Program [ncome Reuse Plan. Administration of Program lncome
pursuant to a plan is considered a "new proceeding" separate from the drafting and
submission of the plan.
5. You may administer and operate the Revolving Loan Fund because this is a..
"new proceeding."
FACTSs
You retired from your state job at HCD on Septemb er 2. 2oog. At HCD you
participated in decisions to award -qrants to certain cities and counties through the CDBG
' Srtme facts recited in this letter are taken fiom your prior requests fbr advice in connectign with
our letters. I-08- 150 and A-09-0-5 l. the CDBG Grants Management Manual. which you have ret-erenced.
and our telephone conversation of September lj.2009.
program. However, while you were employed at HCD, you recused yourself from, and
had no involvement in, funding decisions involving Nevada County and jurisdictions
located within Nevada County.
Program Income Reuse Plans and Prograrn Income under CDBG
The CDBG program is a federal program established to make funds available to
eligible jurisdictions for development of viable communities by providing decent housing
and a suitable living environment and by expanding economic opportunities for persons
of low and moderate income. Under the program, HCD grants, (also referred to as
"CDBG grants"), are awarded to local jurisdictions, which then use the funds to make
loans to various individuals or entities. Proceeds from the repayment of these loans are
considered "Program [ncome." The local jurisdiction may then loan these funds again
pursuant to a Program Income Reuse Plan that must be approved by HCD. Once the plan
is approved, use of the funds is governed by the plan, rather than the original grant
agreement. Typically, the local jurisdiction places Program Income in a Revolving Loan
Fund from which future loans can be made to serve the purposes of the CDBG program.
Nevada County
You began your employment with HCD in 1990 and continued there until 2004,
at which time you went to work full time for Nevada County. Your work for Nevada
County involved writing grant applications for funds provided under the CDBG program
You returned to HCD in2006 and retired on September 1,200g.
Since retiring from state service, you have operated a consulting business, David
Nelson Consulting. Your business has entered into a contract to implement portions of
two existing CDBG grants awarded to Nevada County. Your services would consist of
coordinating and packaging individual loan applications, submitting them to HCD and
communicating with HCD staff regarding these submissions. You would do so by using
a CDBG checklist as a guide. You describe your work under this contract as ministerial.
You ask whether you may assist Nevada County in implementing existing grants under
this contract.
Since leaving state service, you have also provided grant application services for
Nevada County both as an unpaid volunteer and for compensation. Now that these grants
have been awarded, you ask whether you may contract with Nevada county as a
consultant to implement or facilitate these grants and the loans related to awards under
these grants.
Citlt of Di.ron
You would like to contract with the City of Dixon to amend a "Program Income
Reuse Plan" and to administer the "Program lncome" generated from the use of CDBG
firnds in accordance with the plan. The Program Income Reuse Plan must be approved
by HCD, both at the time of initial set-up and upon changing the use of the funds. you


Page No. "1
describe HCD approval to consist of using a checklist of "predetermined" items that are
set forth in the CDBG's Grants Management Manual (the "Manual"). You ask whether
you may assist in the amendment of a Program Income Reuse Plan, administer the related
Program lncome, and administer and operate the resulting Revolving Loan Fund if, as an
employee of HCD, yoLl approved the city's original plan, using the checklist. You also
ask whether yoll may administer the Program Income if someone else handles the
amendment of the plan. You have not indicated whether this other person would be
related to David Nelson Consulting.
